summary: The test suite comprehensively covers all four required functions (Get-DirectoryHashMap, Compare-DirectoryTrees, New-SyncPlan, Invoke-SyncPlan) and both operating modes (dry-run and execute), plus an end-to-end integration test that walks through the full workflow. TDD cycle comments clearly demarcate intent and the structure mirrors the task requirements closely. Design is excellent: BeforeEach/AfterEach cleanup with unique temp directories ensures test isolation, reusable helper functions (New-TestDirectory, New-TestFile) are clean and typed, Describe/Context nesting is logical, and test names are descriptive. The integration test's final assertion — re-hashing both trees and comparing maps — is a strong correctness proof. Rigor is good but has some gaps: no tests for zero-byte/empty files, filenames with spaces or special characters, or paths that don't yet exist as a target root. The error-handling section covers only missing source files for COPY; missing COPY/DELETE for paths with permission errors or a missing target directory root aren't exercised. Strict-mode invariants (parameter type enforcement, OutputType compliance) are also not tested behaviorally. These omissions are the primary reason rigor falls short of a 5.
